Build widgets directly inside your notes.
daily notes should help you run what you thought, not just remember it
Generates disposable widgets inline for one-off tools, trackers, calculators, and experiments
Includes optional AI features for note chat, search, and dry-run edits inside the app
Instead of writing something down and rebuilding the context later in another tool, you stay inside the note and keep moving.
Keep daily planning in one timeline.
Keep everything as plain markdown on disk.
Let unfinished and recurring work come back automatically.
Many tools only need to exist for a day or a week.
Most journaling apps are good at capture and weak at execution.
Philo is built for the gap between capture and execution.
Philo is built to make daily notes lighter, not turn them into another subscription silo.
Philo is designed to collapse that gap.
Philo is licensed under the GNU General Public License v3.0 or later.
Philo is meant to sit beside your vault, not replace it.
Philo is the IDE for your daily notes.
Philo keeps tomorrow, today, and past notes close together so the page already knows what was in flight when you open it.
Philo makes widgets cheap to create, easy to delete, and reusable only when they earn it.
recurring work should reappear automatically
small tools should be cheap to generate right where the note needs them
Supports markdown-native content like images, wiki links, and Excalidraw embeds
The plan is to integrate this daily-notes workflow into Char in the fastrepl/char repo over time.
unfinished tasks should carry forward until they are done
Your notes stay as files you control.
